severity.critical: Confirmed security exposure, data loss risk, broken auth, destructive migration risk, or production-breaking behavior.
severity.high: Likely bug, integrity issue, architecture violation affecting feature work, or missing critical validation.
severity.medium: Maintainability, consistency, performance, or testability issue with clear cost.
severity.low: Style, naming, documentation, or small cleanup issue.

database: normalized transactional schema unless denormalization is justified
consistent primary and foreign key naming
constraints for required, unique, and bounded values
indexes aligned with frequent reads and joins
safe migration and rollback or forward-fix plan
backend: business logic isolated from framework edges
no needless abstraction or pass-through wrappers
tests cover business rules and critical flows
frontend: no duplicate core UI components without reason
framework logic belongs in composables, hooks, services, stores, or utilities rather than bloated pages/components
clear loading, empty, error, and success states
API usage follows project data-loading conventions
security: no secrets or credentials committed to code
SQL, shell, template, and HTML injection risks avoided
sensitive data not leaked in responses, logs, or errors
dependencies reviewed for high and critical vulnerabilities
graphql: schema models domain capabilities rather than leaking database tables directly
queries are side-effect free and mutations return useful payloads
resolvers stay thin and delegate business logic
list fields are paginated or bounded
client operations use generated types when available
utilities: shared helpers have clear ownership and purpose
no duplicate utilities for the same behavior
no custom implementation when a built-in or installed library already solves it well
wrappers add policy, type safety, observability, or error mapping
testing: critical business and user flows covered
regression tests added for fixed bugs
tests assert behavior rather than private implementation details
database and migration risks covered where relevant
flaky or skipped tests have an owner and reason
performance: list endpoints and queries are bounded or paginated
slow external or background work is not blocking requests unnecessarily
observability: important flows have useful logs without sensitive data
request or correlation IDs are preserved where supported
critical jobs and external calls expose failures
metrics and alerts map to user impact or operational risk
delivery: CI runs the project's required quality checks
database migrations are deploy-safe or clearly flagged
feature flags have owners and cleanup plans
deployments do not depend on hidden manual steps
project_context: target architecture decisions are recorded as ADRs
project docs guide future agents without duplicating code
known legacy gaps are tracked in a migration backlog
audit inventory records health score, formula, and trend
drift report verifies docs against current code
CI quality gate checks diffs against accepted project context

finding_shape.title: concise problem statement
finding_shape.validation: test, migration check, lint, typecheck, or manual verification
